title: Hieracium perasperum
uses: Hieracium perasperum has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues. The plant's leaves and stems are said to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help in soothing skin irritations and wounds. In some cultures, Hieracium perasperum is used as a natural remedy for treating digestive problems and fever. However, more research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of its medicinal uses. Its potential health benefits make it a subject of interest in modern herbalism and pharmacology.
taxonomy: Hieracium perasperum is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe within the sunflower family (Asteraceae). It belongs to the genus Hieracium, which consists of over 1000 species of hawkweeds. The genus Hieracium is part of the larger clade of flowering plants known as angiosperms. As part of the Asteraceae family, Hieracium perasperum shares characteristics with other dandelion tribe members, such as dandelions and hawkweeds. This classification places Hieracium perasperum within a larger group of flowering plants with similar characteristics and evolutionary relationships.

intro: Hieracium perasperum, also known as rough hawkweed, is a perennial plant species within the dandelion family (Asteraceae) characterized by its yellow flowers and bitter foliage. The health benefits of Hieracium perasperum include its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various health conditions, such as arthritis and skin disorders. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium perasperum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditional herbal preparations made with Hieracium perasperum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to support digestive health and reduce inflammation.
